calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-24-2011 Assigned To Trial Court

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-24-2011 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Court Trial/Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Ellen Chaitin, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, Reporter: B. Fields, CSR #7235.

Scott Freedman, Esq. for Plaintiff: Baner Financial interests.
Plaintiff's Witness William Baner was placed under oath and direct examined.
No appearance by defendant Richard Leslie, and no appearance by the Defaulted Co-Defendant Matheson Equity, a Delaware limited liability company.

Exhibit 1 - Lease agreement ----------ID/EVID
Exhibit 2 - Payroll and Invoice ------ID/EVID
Exhibit 3 - Copy of two checks --------ID/EVID

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff and against defendants for Judgment as follows:

Damages - $52,311.00
Prejudgment interest - $7,998.60
Attorney fees - $9,727.60
Costs - $2,067.95
Total - $72,105.15
Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-14-2011 Off Calendar

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-20-2010 Continued To Jan-14-11 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 502

Notice Of Motion For The Imposition Of Terminating Or Evidentiary Sanctions; For A Further Award Of Monetary Sanctions As Against Deft Leslie
Off Calendar Jan-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SCOTT A. FREEDMAN OF THE ZACKS & UTRECHT LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 956-8100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF BANER FINANCIAL INTERESTS, Motion For The Imposition Of Terminating Or Evidentiary Sanctions; For A Further Award Of Monetary Sanctions As Against Defendant Leslie, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHALL BE PRECLUDED AT TRIAL FROM INTRODUCING 1) ANY DOCUMENTS OR OTHER EVIDENCE THAT WOULD SUPPORT ANY OF THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES STATED IN THE ANSWER FILED IN THIS MATTER 3/23/10; (2) ANY DOCUMENTS OR OTHER EVIDENCE THAT WOULD SUPPORT THE DENIAL OF ANY MATERIAL ALLEGATIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT FILED IN THIS MATTER 2/23/10; (3) ANY EVIDENCE THAT WOULD HAVE SUPPORTED THE DENIAL OF RFA'S NUMBERS 1-4, 8-11, 13-14, 16-24 AND 27-28 THAT WERE SERVED 7/1/10. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO PAY $490.00 IN SANCTIONS IN ADDITION TO THE $565 PREVIOUSLY AWARDED BY THIS COURT ON 10/26/10. NO OPPOSITION FILED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Not reported. =301/PJB
